stories.headline: Anthropic ships Claude Code Channels: control live AI coding sessions from Telegram and Discord on mobile
stories.summary: Released March 20 as a research preview, Claude Code Channels enables developers to message a live Claude Code session running on their desktop from Telegram or Discord on their phone — the agent responds back after executing code, running tests, or fixing bugs with full filesystem, MCP, and git access. The plugin-based architecture already has community requests for Slack, WhatsApp, and iMessage, positioning it as Anthropic's direct answer to OpenClaw's mobile-first headstart in the async developer agent space.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to ship-to-shore radio teletype protocols
stories.lateral_explanation: Ship-to-shore radio teletype systems in the 1930s solved exactly this problem: a captain needed to issue and receive orders without returning to port, so operators developed asynchronous relay protocols where messages queued aboard the ship, executed locally, and acknowledgments returned through the same channel. Claude Code Channels imports this architecture wholesale: the developer is the remote operator, the running Claude session is the autonomous vessel, and Telegram is the teletype channel.

stories.headline: Alibaba's Qwen 3.5 Small: 9B model matches 120B performance and runs offline on iPhone
stories.summary: Alibaba released the Qwen 3.5 Small series, a family of open-source models from 0.8B to 9B parameters that match models eight times their size on reasoning benchmarks — with the 4B variant excelling at agent tasks and the 9B running on-device on recent iPhones in offline mode. Built on a hybrid Gated Delta Network with sparse MoE architecture, the series permanently shifts the calculus on whether production AI deployments need cloud infrastructure or can live entirely on owned hardware.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Foster's Rule of island dwarfism
stories.lateral_explanation: Foster's Rule describes the evolutionary tendency for large animals stranded on islands to shrink dramatically because resource scarcity rewards metabolic efficiency over brute size: island elephants became dwarf mammoths, island hippos became miniature. The Qwen 3.5 series follows an analogous pressure — deployment constraint (edge hardware, privacy, offline operation) — and the result is models that produce surprising capability density at small parameter counts. The 9B matching 120B performance is the mammalian equivalent of a 200kg island elephant doing everything a 5-ton mainland elephant can do.

stories.headline: MiroThinker 72B reaches 81.9% GAIA through 'interaction scaling' — 600 tool calls per task, fully open-source
stories.summary: MiroLab's open-source 72B research agent introduces interaction scaling as a third performance dimension alongside model size and context length — using RL to train the model to execute up to 600 tool calls per task in sustained multi-turn reasoning chains approaching paid GPT-5 performance on GAIA (81.9%) while remaining fully open-source. The result directly challenges the assumption that research-grade AI agents require proprietary closed models or frontier compute budgets.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Socratic elenchus dialogue method
stories.lateral_explanation: Socrates' elenchus was not about what he knew but about how many iterations of question-and-answer he ran: each exchange exposed an assumption, tested it, surfaced a contradiction, and restarted from a cleaner premise. MiroThinker's interaction scaling is structurally identical: the model is trained to run cycles of tool call, observation, and revised query — the intelligence emerges from the number of verification loops, not from static knowledge. Socrates was smarter at the end of a five-hour conversation than at the beginning, not because he had read more books.

stories.headline: Apple iOS 26.4 ships Gemini-powered Siri with on-screen context awareness — reads whatever is on your display
stories.summary: Apple confirmed iOS 26.4 will ship with Siri rebuilt on Google's Gemini models under a multi-year partnership, introducing on-screen context awareness that lets Siri read the content currently displayed in any app — if a restaurant appears in Safari, Siri can make the reservation without the user copying the name or switching context. The transformation turns Siri from a command-response utility into a context-aware agent capable of chaining multi-step actions from a single natural language request.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to colonial governor's secretarial proxy system
stories.lateral_explanation: In colonial administration, the governor's secretary held power exceeding their formal title — the secretary decided which petitions reached the desk, framed them with context, and often drafted the response. The secretary's contextual knowledge of what the governor was currently looking at made the entire administration more effective than if every interaction required explicit instruction. Siri's on-screen awareness gives it exactly this positioning: it knows what you're looking at, so it can act as a capable intermediary rather than a command system requiring you to describe what you've already seen.

stories.headline: Kimi K2.5 on Cloudflare edge: 256K agentic context for any developer without managing servers
stories.summary: Moonshot AI's Kimi K2.5 — with a 256,000 token context window optimized for multi-step agentic tasks — is now available through Cloudflare Workers AI for edge deployment, giving any developer access to frontier-level long-context reasoning without managing infrastructure. Cloudflare simultaneously shipped Bonk, an automated GitHub code review agent built on Kimi K2.5 that serves teams without dedicated reviewers.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Roman annona grain distribution network
stories.lateral_explanation: The Roman annona — feeding 1.2 million people in imperial Rome — worked by placing warehouses at Ostia harbor and along major roads, routing provisioning requests to the nearest available stock. The key insight was that decentralization reduced latency: you didn't need to go to Rome to get Roman grain. Kimi K2.5 on Cloudflare edge replicates this for AI inference: frontier-level reasoning is now available at the network edge closest to the request, without routing latency to a centralized data center.

stories.headline: Anthropic launches Cowork persistent agent thread for Claude Desktop and mobile — Max plans today, Pro in 48h
stories.summary: Anthropic released the persistent agent thread for Cowork mode starting March 23 for Max plan users, enabling a long-running Claude Desktop agent that manages files and tasks across sessions without coding or configuration — continuing context between conversations the way a human assistant maintains awareness of ongoing projects. The release makes Cowork Anthropic's consumer-facing answer to the no-code AI workflow space, running natively on the user's desktop.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Japanese genkan threshold ritual
stories.lateral_explanation: The Japanese genkan — the threshold space between outside and inside in a traditional home — is not simply an entrance but a transition ritual: you remove your shoes, change your posture, and signal a different mode of attention before entering the interior. A persistent agent thread creates an analogous cognitive threshold: rather than each conversation starting from scratch, the agent maintains accumulated context of ongoing projects, so transitioning to 'working with AI' requires no orientation overhead. What changes is not the agent's capability but its positional awareness — it always knows where the house is.

stories.headline: Alibaba launches Wukong enterprise AI platform: coordinated agents for 20M DingTalk users, Slack and Teams next
stories.summary: Alibaba launched Wukong on March 17, an enterprise AI agent platform that autonomously handles document editing, approval routing, meeting transcription, and business research through DingTalk's 20+ million corporate users — with Slack, Teams, and WeChat integrations on the roadmap. Wukong signals Alibaba's strategic pivot: enterprise agent infrastructure, not model releases, is the revenue-generating frontier.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Mongol Empire Yam postal relay system
stories.lateral_explanation: The Mongol Yam system worked by placing relay stations every 25-40km across the steppe, each stocked with fresh horses and riders. A message from Beijing could reach the western empire edge in days not weeks because the system eliminated the bottleneck of fatigue by relaying continuously. Wukong's architecture imports this logic: 20 million users aren't adopting a new AI model but connecting to an agent relay layer that picks up any workflow task, executes it without fatigue, and passes the result forward.

stories.headline: Listen Labs raises $69M after 1M+ AI interviews: Microsoft cuts customer research wait from weeks to hours
stories.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B led by Ribbit Capital after scaling 15x ARR in nine months by replacing surveys and focus groups with AI-moderated customer conversations running simultaneously across every segment — Microsoft cut its research cycle from weeks to hours. The company's $500M valuation represents the first clearly documented case of AI replacing a knowledge work function at the scale of one million conversations.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Delphic oracle consultation model
stories.lateral_explanation: The Delphic oracle's power came not from divine insight but from being the single point where information patterns of thousands of simultaneous consultations accumulated into detectable signals about which strategies succeeded. Listen Labs operates by the same structural logic: the platform gains predictive power not from any single interview but from running every consultation simultaneously, building a pattern-recognition layer no individual enterprise research function can replicate alone.

stories.headline: Mistral Small 3.1 24B: production-grade open-weight multimodal at $0.03/M input with 128K context
stories.summary: Mistral released Small 3.1 24B with vision capabilities, a 128K context window, and input pricing at $0.03/M tokens — significantly below GPT-5.4 mini — positioning it as a production-grade option for enterprises needing frontier multimodal performance without proprietary model lock-in. Each month the performance threshold for 'good enough for production' moves further down the open-source ladder.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Bessemer steel process commoditization
stories.lateral_explanation: When Henry Bessemer patented his steel-making process in 1856, wrought iron had been dominant for centuries — expensive and limiting what could be built at scale. The Bessemer converter cut production time from days to 15 minutes and cost by 90%, collapsing iron's economic moat overnight. Mistral Small 3.1 at $0.03/M applies the same pressure to AI inference: the moment production-grade multimodal reasoning becomes cheaper than a dedicated analytics team, the cost justification for proprietary models collapses exactly as iron's did when steel became cheap enough to build bridges.

stories.headline: CUDA Agent: open-source GPU kernel optimizer outperforms GPT-5.4 and Claude on hardware-specific code
stories.summary: Independent developers shipped CUDA Agent, a specialized open-source tool that writes and optimizes GPU kernels more effectively than GPT-5.4 and Claude on hardware-specific tasks — showing that generalist frontier models are systematically outclassed by narrow specialist agents trained on domain-specific reward signals. The pattern mirrors protein folding: meaningful AI progress comes not from larger general models but from agents fine-tuned on the specific feedback loops of a given domain.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to medieval guild mastery vs journeyman stratification
stories.lateral_explanation: Medieval craft guilds created a three-tier system specifically because general capability was insufficient for the most demanding work: a journeyman could execute standard pieces, but a master handled edge cases requiring domain expertise built from years of specific feedback. CUDA Agent shows this stratification reproducing in AI: GPT-5.4 is the skilled journeyman, but domain-specific agents trained on GPU optimization reward signals are the masters — their training signal is narrower and more precise, producing work the generalist cannot match.

stories.headline: Workday integrates Sana as its AI front door: all data, all workflows, any employee — no code required
stories.summary: Workday announced Sana as its new unified AI infrastructure layer, giving all Workday customers a conversational interface to ask questions, run reports, and invoke transactions directly against enterprise data without navigating complex menus — while automatically respecting each employee's security permissions. The Agent Development Studio lets employees build internal workflow apps using drag-and-drop tools without requiring engineering resources.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Norman Exchequer administrative reform
stories.lateral_explanation: William I's Norman administration introduced the Exchequer as a way to make the complex finances of a conquered kingdom legible to administrators who couldn't trust the oral reports of local sheriffs. The Exchequer didn't give the king more power; it gave him access to information already existing but opaque to the center. Workday's Sana interface does the same thing to enterprise data: the information always existed in Workday's database, embedded in navigation hierarchies requiring training to access. The conversational layer is the Exchequer cloth — it makes existing information legible without changing what's stored.

stories.headline: Alibaba Wukong ships to 20M enterprise users: agents edit docs, route approvals, and transcribe meetings autonomously
stories.summary: Alibaba's Wukong platform launched March 17 with agents that autonomously handle document editing, approval routing, meeting transcription, and business research — operating continuously through DingTalk's 20+ million corporate user base without requiring structured inputs or human prompting at each step. The platform's emergence from a complete infrastructure rewrite marks Alibaba's clearest strategic pivot: enterprise agent fleets, not communication tools, are the next growth bet.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Dutch East India Company VOC centralized trade network
stories.lateral_explanation: The Dutch East India Company solved a specific coordination problem: how do you manage thousands of trading agents operating simultaneously across three oceans, each making autonomous decisions, without coordination collapsing into chaos or corruption? Their answer was standardized procedures embedded in company training and charter, combined with central reporting back to Amsterdam. Wukong's coordinated agent fleet faces the same problem: 20 million users generating autonomous agent actions need a governance layer maintaining coherence across simultaneous workflows.

stories.headline: Salesforce Slackbot GA: role-specific AI agent reads your work context and executes across sales, service, engineering
stories.summary: Salesforce's upgraded Slackbot became generally available in March as a personal AI agent that learns individual work patterns by reading messages, files, and channel access — then executing role-specific workflows for sales, marketing, engineering, service, and operations without leaving Slack. Built on Anthropic's Claude and integrated with Agentforce, the agent handles a complete task layer within the interface where most enterprise knowledge work already happens.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Japanese nemawashi consensus-building practice
stories.lateral_explanation: Japanese nemawashi — 'going around the roots' — describes the consensus-building process before a formal decision: you brief each stakeholder individually, incorporate their concerns, and only call a meeting when everyone has already agreed. The process acknowledges that formal meeting time is too valuable for information transfer. Salesforce Slackbot embeds nemawashi logic by surfacing CRM context, draft responses, and project history before the meeting happens — so every formal interaction is already prefaced by the preparation that Japanese organizations build manually.

stories.headline: ServiceNow AI Gateway March 2026: AI Steward enforcement goes live, MCP servers now centrally governed
stories.summary: ServiceNow's March 2026 AI Gateway update activated enforcement of AI Steward approval decisions enterprise-wide, with automated client registration via CIMD and per-toggle PII detection for individual MCP servers — meaning IT governance teams now control which AI models access which workflows, with full audit trails. The update marks the moment enterprise AI governance shifted from advisory to operational.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to medieval sumptuary law enforcement
stories.lateral_explanation: Medieval sumptuary laws regulating garments for different social ranks were not about aesthetics but about information legibility: in a pre-literate society, visible markers of rank had to be legally enforced to remain readable, or the social order became ambiguous. ServiceNow's AI Steward enforcement operates on the same logic: in a system where thousands of AI agents can theoretically access any workflow, the governance layer's job is to make access control visible and auditable — to ensure the rank hierarchy of which agents can do what remains readable to the humans governing the system.

stories.headline: Listen Labs AI interviews replace 1M surveys: Microsoft cuts customer research cycle from weeks to hours
stories.summary: Listen Labs has conducted over one million AI-moderated customer interviews for enterprises including Microsoft, Sweetgreen, Perplexity, and Robinhood — replacing research cycles that previously took weeks with results in hours by running qualitative interviews with every segment simultaneously. The $500M valuation and 15x ARR growth in nine months represent the first clearly documented case of AI replacing an entire knowledge work function at enterprise scale.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Dewey Decimal System accessibility breakthrough
stories.lateral_explanation: The Dewey Decimal Classification system transformed public libraries not by creating new knowledge but by making existing knowledge findable — the insight was that accessibility was the bottleneck, not content creation. Listen Labs inverts this: in customer research, the bottleneck was never access to willing respondents but the cost and time of synthesizing qualitative responses at scale. AI moderated interviews remove the synthesis bottleneck the same way Dewey removed the findability bottleneck: the knowledge was always there, the constraint was the process for extracting it.

stories.headline: XPENG VLA 2.0 rolls out Level 4 autonomy to customer vehicles — first physical AI agent at mass consumer scale
stories.summary: XPENG began rolling out VLA 2.0 to existing customer vehicles in March 2026, making it the first Level 4 autonomous driving system deployed to consumer cars at scale rather than in controlled pilot zones. VLA 2.0 processes unstructured real-world environments through a multimodal reasoning model rather than predefined rules — marking the moment AI agents crossed from desktop workflows into physical autonomy deployed to millions of road users.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to proprioception development in surgical training
stories.lateral_explanation: Surgical training research documents that surgeons practicing laparoscopic procedures using simulation showed faster learning curves in actual operations — not because simulation was more realistic but because it created a deliberate feedback loop between motor action and outcome that the body internalizes as proprioceptive memory. VLA 2.0's deployment creates the same feedback architecture at automotive scale: each edge case encountered in real-world driving becomes training signal for the next version, creating proprioceptive memory no simulation environment can pre-construct.

stories.headline: CXAI launches Zero-Touch Campus Deployment: AI automates enterprise workplace onboarding across global offices
stories.summary: CXAI launched its AI-powered Zero-Touch Campus Deployment Platform on March 17, automating the full sequence of new employee workplace onboarding — access provisioning, device setup, navigation, and facility orientation — across global corporate campuses without manual IT intervention. The platform represents the shift of AI from individual productivity tools to infrastructure-layer systems that operate entire physical work environments.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Bentham's panopticon architectural theory
stories.lateral_explanation: Jeremy Bentham's panopticon was not primarily about surveillance but about behavioral normalization through implied observation: inmates could never know when they were being watched, so they behaved as if always watched. CXAI's Zero-Touch Campus creates an analogous structure: AI-driven analytics continuously monitor access patterns, workflow completions, and facility usage across every touchpoint, creating a system where organizational behavior is always legible to the platform without requiring individual human supervisors. The functional difference from Bentham's panopticon is that the observed subjects are processes, not prisoners.

stories.headline: Samsung Galaxy S26 'Now Nudge': agentic AI predicts your next action before you ask — 1.4 billion devices
stories.summary: Samsung launched Galaxy S26 with Now Nudge, an on-device agentic feature that learns behavioral patterns and proactively suggests actions — restaurant reservations, follow-up messages, calendar rescheduling — before users search for what to do, running alongside Gemini and Perplexity agents. With the global release, agentic AI that anticipates rather than responds will be in more than 1.4 billion active Samsung devices by Q2 2026.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Victorian domestic service anticipatory duty
stories.lateral_explanation: The Victorian butler's primary skill was anticipation — the highest compliment was that guests found what they needed before they knew they needed it: the fresh drink appeared as the glass emptied, the carriage ordered before the conversation about leaving concluded. This anticipatory service was not telepathy but pattern recognition accumulated over years of observing the same household's behavioral rhythms. Samsung's Now Nudge imports this model: the intelligence is not in answering requests but in recognizing the moment before the request forms, which requires exactly the longitudinal behavioral memory that made the Victorian butler irreplaceable.

stories.headline: The enterprise AI OS war: Salesforce, ServiceNow, Workday, SAP, and Oracle all claim the same stack
stories.summary: Salesforce, ServiceNow, Workday, SAP, and Oracle are simultaneously marketing their platform as the unified enterprise AI operating system — creating a winner-take-most battle for the agent orchestration layer across HR, CRM, ITSM, and ERP. The competitive dynamic mirrors the 2010s mobile platform war: the near-term outcome isn't one winner but a messy multi-vendor integration problem until one platform achieves enough cross-function adoption to become the default.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Betamax vs VHS format war distribution logic
stories.lateral_explanation: The Betamax vs VHS format war is remembered as a technical contest, but the decisive factor was not quality — Betamax was arguably superior — but distribution: VHS had longer tape capacity matching rental feature film length, which drove video store adoption, which drove consumer adoption, which locked in the standard through installed base. The enterprise AI OS war follows the same logic: technical capabilities matter less than which platform first achieves critical mass in cross-function enterprise adoption, because the standard will be set by whoever first becomes the assumed integration point across HR, CRM, and ITSM simultaneously.

stories.headline: PwC: 79% of enterprises run AI agents in 2026 — most migration is from legacy RPA to agentic architectures
stories.summary: PwC survey data shows 79% of enterprises are already running AI agents in 2026, with most deployments migrating from rules-based RPA to agentic architectures because legacy automation cannot handle the exception volume in complex operational workflows. Finance reports cost reductions up to 70%; HR cuts onboarding cycle times by up to 80%; agentic sales deployments show 4x to 7x conversion improvements.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to English enclosure movement labor displacement
stories.lateral_explanation: The English enclosure movement converted open common land to enclosed private farms not by eliminating farming but by changing who controlled the workflow — the same physical labor was required, but now organized through landlord instruction rather than customary practice, displacing thousands who had performed identical tasks under the old system. The migration from RPA to agentic architectures is structurally analogous: the enterprise workflows are the same, but agentic AI controls the decision layer that RPA could not, displacing the human judgment layer embedded in every rule-based automation exception path.

stories.headline: Café Pombo, retrato de un Madrid que ya no existe: Telemadrid documenta la tertulia de Ramón Gómez de la Serna
stories.summary: Telemadrid dedicó en febrero un reportaje al Café de la Calle Carretas donde Ramón Gómez de la Serna organizó durante décadas la tertulia más influyente del Madrid de principios del siglo XX — reuniendo a la vanguardia española en un formato híbrido entre conferencia, performance y conversación que él mismo inventó. Del edificio ya no queda nada salvo una inscripción en el suelo, pero el modelo de tertulia como 'formato narrativo con estilo propio' es el antepasado directo del podcast de ideas y el club de lectura como experiencia de comunidad.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Japanese ukiyo-e floating world social hubs
stories.lateral_explanation: The ukiyo-e tradition documented the social life of Edo's theaters and pleasure districts not as decadent escapism but as the nodes of a genuine civic culture: spaces outside institutional hierarchy where merchants, artisans, and intellectuals mixed freely. Café Pombo occupied the same sociological position in early 20th century Madrid: a space outside academic and political institutions where the emerging avant-garde defined its identity through performance, argument, and mutual recognition. Both the ukiyo-e world and Pombo's tertulia were 'floating' precisely because they were not anchored to any fixed institution — their value was in their ephemerality and openness.

stories.headline: El gitano por amor en el Teatro de la Zarzuela: ópera de Cervantes no representada en 200 años (25 marzo–12 abril)
stories.summary: El Teatro de la Zarzuela estrena esta semana la recuperación de El gitano por amor, ópera buffa de Manuel García basada en La gitanilla de Cervantes — compuesta en 1828 con libreto en español y sin representarse durante casi 200 años hasta ser rescatada por el Estudio de Ópera de Málaga y premiada con los Ópera XXI. La producción de Emilio Sagi lleva a escena tres siglos de cadena de transmisión literaria: una novela de 1613, convertida en ópera en 1828, recuperada en 2026.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Homeric oral tradition transmission archaeology
stories.lateral_explanation: Homeric scholarship reconstructed how the Iliad and Odyssey were transmitted not through written texts but through generations of oral singers who each held a version in memory, adapting it to local audiences while preserving structural features. El gitano por amor undergoes an analogous transmission: Cervantes' La gitanilla (1613) becomes Manuel García's opera (1828), then 200 years of unperformed manuscript, then the 2026 Zarzuela production. Each transmission is a recomposition, not a copy — what the Teatro de la Zarzuela is performing is not Cervantes but the accumulated decisions of everyone who held the story between then and now.

stories.headline: Tertulia Literaria Rafael Montesinos — Versos Entrevistados: poesía como conversación viva en el Retiro (24 marzo)
stories.summary: La Tertulia Literaria Hispanoamericana 'Rafael Montesinos' — uno de los ciclos literarios más longevos de Madrid, activo en la Biblioteca Eugenio Trías del Retiro — programa mañana una sesión de 'Versos Entrevistados' con el poeta Jesús Urceloy y el editor Jorge Ortega, explorando el poema como espacio de conversación más que de monólogo. La tertulia lleva el nombre del poeta sevillano cronista del Madrid romántico y amigo personal de Luis Cernuda.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Platonic symposium dialogue structure
stories.lateral_explanation: The Platonic symposium had a specific structure where Socrates intervened not to conclude but to expose the inadequacy of what had been said, reopening the question rather than closing it. The form was designed to prevent premature closure — the goal was not consensus but sustained collective inquiry. The tertulia literaria as Ramón Gómez de la Serna designed it (and as the Rafael Montesinos circle continues it) operates on the same premise: the poem is not an answer but a provocation that the gathering talks around, ensuring the question remains open at the end of the evening.

stories.headline: Presentación de Cautivos: El tesoro de Cervantes y Dalí en la Biblioteca del Retiro (26 marzo)
stories.summary: El 26 de marzo se presenta en la Biblioteca Eugenio Trías la novela Cautivos: El tesoro de Cervantes y Dalí de Jesús Lázaro Docio, obra que entrelaza el período de Cervantes como cautivo en Argel (1575–1580) — los cinco años que más marcaron su escritura posterior — con la figura de Salvador Dalí y un misterio construido sobre el archivo de ambos. La presentación reúne las dos herencias culturales más identificadas con el carácter español: el escritor del siglo XVII y el artista que lo representó en el XX.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Thomas Browne's Pseudodoxia Epidemica methodology
stories.lateral_explanation: Thomas Browne's Pseudodoxia Epidemica (1646) was a book about the gap between what is commonly believed and what is actually known: its method was to take each received truth, trace its transmission history, and show how error accumulates through confident repetition. Cautivos applies the same epistemological pressure to cultural mythology: what do we actually know about Cervantes' captivity in Algiers versus what has been retold so many times it feels like certainty? The novel's pairing of Cervantes and Dalí — two figures whose biographies have been both documented and mythologized — is a test of how much a culture knows versus how much it merely remembers.

stories.headline: XIX Ciclo 'Mujeres al Órgano' abre mañana en San Ginés: el instrumento que definió el sonido de Madrid durante cuatro siglos
stories.summary: El XIX Ciclo de Música de Órgano de San Ginés 'Mujeres al Órgano' abre mañana con intérpretes que recuperan el repertorio de composición e improvisación de órgano en tradición española y europea — un instrumento que durante cuatro siglos definió la vida acústica de Madrid y del que quedan menos de veinte instrumentos históricos en uso activo. El ciclo conecta la arquitectura sonora de la Semana Santa con una tradición de interpretación femenina que en el siglo XVII vivía mayoritariamente en conventos clausurados que aún estructuran el callejero del centro.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Ottoman fasıl music gendered performance tradition
stories.lateral_explanation: The Ottoman fasıl was explicitly gendered in its institutional setting: the court fasıl was performed exclusively by male ensembles, while distinct female performance traditions developed in the harem and later in secular contexts. Spanish sacred organ tradition followed a structurally parallel history: public church organists were male by institutional rule, while female organists developed their practice within convents — which is exactly the institutional geography this cycle makes visible. Both traditions reveal how much musical culture was shaped not by talent distribution but by which physical spaces women were permitted to inhabit.

books.title: Co-Intelligence: Living and Working with AI
books.author: Ethan Mollick
books.why: Mollick — Wharton professor and one of David's monitored sources — is the most evidence-based writer on how real professionals are changing how they work with AI. After a week of stories about Workday Sana, Salesforce Slackbot, and Samsung Now Nudge all embedding AI into daily work routines, this book gives the conceptual framework for what 'working with AI' actually means at the individual level versus the enterprise infrastructure level.

books.title: AI Engineering: Building Applications with Foundation Models
books.author: Chip Huyen
books.why: Huyen — one of David's monitored sources and author of the most-read O'Reilly book since release — wrote the definitive guide to building production AI applications. This week's stories about CUDA Agent specialization, MiroThinker interaction scaling, and Qwen 3.5 edge deployment all point to the same theme: AI engineering is becoming about composing specialized systems, not selecting the biggest model. Huyen's framework for evaluation, deployment, and reliability is the missing technical context.
